title: Macro Signals
deck: A static US macro dashboard compiled from official public datasets. The build pipeline refreshes the data first, then ships a simple front-end bundle that can live on GitHub Pages or another static host.
highlights: Payrolls changed by 178k in Mar 26, while headline CPI ran 3.3%.
Real GDP growth last printed at 0.5% and fed funds averaged 3.6%.
The 10Y-2Y spread was 0.5 pp and BIS private credit was 140.4% of GDP.

sections.title: Monthly pulse
sections.description: High-frequency labor and inflation series come directly from BLS so the dashboard can highlight current-cycle momentum without scraping webpages.

sections.title: Real-economy cycle
sections.description: Growth, production, and housing are shown with chart types that match their release cadence and economic meaning.

sections.title: Policy and curve
sections.description: Rates are shown as lines because the level and the persistence of the regime matter more than single-point changes.

sections.title: Balance-sheet backdrop
sections.description: BIS leverage series and World Bank annual context help separate short-cycle moves from slower structural pressure.

indicators.title: Unemployment rate
indicators.rationale: A line chart is best for the unemployment rate because turning points and persistence matter more than one-off monthly moves.

indicators.title: Nonfarm payroll change
indicators.description: Bars show month-over-month payroll additions or losses, with the latest print at 178k in Mar 26.
indicators.rationale: Payroll changes are discrete monthly flows, so bars communicate acceleration and reversals better than a level line.

indicators.title: Headline vs. core CPI
indicators.description: Headline CPI ran 3.3% year over year in Mar 26; the dashed line tracks core CPI at 2.7%.
indicators.rationale: Two lines are the clearest way to compare whether broad inflation and underlying inflation are diverging or converging.

indicators.title: Real GDP growth
indicators.description: Quarterly real GDP growth last printed at 0.5% in Q4 25.
indicators.rationale: Quarterly growth prints are discrete observations, so bars make quarter-to-quarter swings easier to compare.

indicators.title: Industrial production
indicators.description: Industrial production stood at 102.6 in Feb 26.
indicators.rationale: The production index is a continuous level series, so a line best shows trend direction and cycle turning points.

indicators.title: Housing starts
indicators.description: Housing starts were running at 1.49M annualized units in Jan 26.
indicators.rationale: An area chart emphasizes the scale of the housing cycle while still making directional changes easy to read.

indicators.title: Effective fed funds rate
indicators.description: The effective fed funds rate averaged 3.6% in Mar 26.
indicators.rationale: Policy rate changes are best tracked as a line because persistence at a given level matters more than any single print.

indicators.title: 10Y minus 2Y Treasury spread
indicators.description: The latest 10Y-2Y spread was 0.5 pp on Apr 10.
indicators.rationale: A line with a zero reference is the clearest way to spot inversions and re-steepening in the yield curve.

indicators.title: Private-sector debt service ratio
indicators.description: The BIS private-sector debt service ratio stood at 14.1% in Q3 25.
indicators.rationale: Debt-service pressure is best read as a continuous quarterly line because its persistence matters more than single-quarter noise.

indicators.title: Private credit to GDP
indicators.description: US private non-financial credit was 140.4% of GDP in Q3 25.
indicators.rationale: An area chart emphasizes the stock-like nature of leverage relative to GDP while keeping the long-run trend readable.

indicators.title: World Bank annual GDP growth
indicators.description: The latest complete World Bank GDP growth estimate for the US is 2.8% in 2024.
indicators.rationale: Annual growth prints are discrete year-by-year observations, so bars are easier to compare than a smoothed line.

indicators.title: World Bank central government debt
indicators.description: Central government debt was 118.0% of GDP in 2024.
indicators.rationale: Debt ratios evolve gradually, so a line is the clearest way to show the medium-run trend.

sourceStatus.name: IMF
sourceStatus.detail: As of 2026-04-13, the IMF portal redirected anonymous API traffic to sign-in and the legacy dataservices endpoint was not reachable from this build environment. Add a working authenticated IMF export or API path if you want IMF included.

sourceStatus.name: BLS
sourceStatus.detail: BLS API refresh failed in this build (BLS request failed with status REQUEST_NOT_PROCESSED); the dashboard is temporarily using current BLS-origin series carried through FRED instead.

sourceStatus.name: FRED
sourceStatus.detail: Using the public FRED CSV export path because FRED_API_KEY is unset.

sourceStatus.name: BIS
sourceStatus.detail: Using the BIS bulk-download portal for quarterly leverage datasets.

sourceStatus.name: World Bank
sourceStatus.detail: Direct World Bank API pull for annual long-run context.

notes: The site is static: all source calls happen at build time, and the browser only reads a compiled JSON file.
FRED uses the official API when FRED_API_KEY is present and falls back to the public CSV export path, with a curl retry path to protect CI builds when fetch is flaky.
FRED-carried series still show their original agency in the UI, including BEA, the Census Bureau, and the Board of Governors.
BLS history is fetched in public 10-year windows so the latest 2026 monthly releases still land even when the full lookback window is longer.
IMF is marked optional because the public portal redirected to sign-in on 2026-04-13.
